Interface ISudokuGrid

All Superinterfaces:
Cloneable
All Known Implementing Classes:
SudokuGrid
public interface ISudokuGrid
Represents a Sudoku grid, providing higher-level operations on top of the basic ISudokuState cell access.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    DEFAULT_SIZE

    Determines whether or not the current state of the sudoku grid is a valid solution so far.

    Empties the sudoku grid of values.

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT_SIZE

      static final int DEFAULT_SIZE
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • validateGridSize

      static void validateGridSize(int size)
      Validates the size of a sudoku grid.

      Sizes supported are all values x, where 2 <= x <= 16, and x is either even, square, or both.

      Parameters:
      size - The size to check.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the size given is not valid.

    • getValue

      int getValue(int row, int column)
      Gets the current value of a cell in a sudoku grid.

      Coordinates are indexed from (0, 0), which is the upper left-most cell in the grid.

      Parameters:
      row - The row, or y-coordinate, of the cell
      column - The column, or x-coordinate, of the cell
      Returns:
      The value currently stored at the cell, or -1 if the cell is empty.

    • setValue

      void setValue(int row, int column, int value)
      Sets the value of a cell in a sudoku grid.

      Coordinates are indexed from (0, 0), which is the upper left-most cell in the grid.

      Note that 1 <= value <= grid size, or to set the cell as empty, value = -1

      Parameters:
      row - The row, or y-coordinate, of the cell
      column - The column, or x-coordinate, of the cell
      value - The value of the cell

    • isValid

      boolean isValid()
      Determines whether or not the current state of the sudoku grid is a valid solution so far.
      Returns:
      true if the grid is valid; false if there are any incompatible cells.

    • isSolved

      boolean isSolved()
      Determines whether or not the sudoku grid has yet been solved.
      Returns:
      true if the grid has been solved; false if there are any incompatible or empty cells.

    • resetGrid

      void resetGrid()
      Empties the sudoku grid of values.

    • getSize

      int getSize()
      Gets the size of the grid.

      The size of a grid is determined as the range of values which it can contain.

      A grid's size can range between 2-16.

      Returns:
      the size of the grid.

    • clone

      ISudokuGrid clone()

    • checkForDuplicates

      boolean checkForDuplicates(int row, int column)
      Checks whether duplicates of the given cell value exist within the ISudokuState which contains it.

      If this state contains another ISudokuState, the method must check within the smallest (deepest) ISudokuState which contains the cell.

      Parameters:
      row - The row (or y-coordinate) of the target cell.
      column - The column (or x-coordinate) of the target cell.
      Returns:
      whether any duplicates of the value at the given coordinates exist within the smallest ISudokuState which contains it.

    • getGridDimensions

      Tuple2<Integer,Integer> getGridDimensions()
      Gets the size of the grid in terms of the number of cells it contains.
      Returns:
      a tuple containing the number of rows and columns in the grid respectively.
